intent: Huge ruleset (212 valid rules: 4 control + 200 single-file watches + 8 syscall). Tests parse performance, volume RANKING (one execve rule dominates), and that output does NOT flood with all 212 lines equally weighted (f3 section 5.1).

rules.note: DOMINANT rule; outweighs all 180 watches combined.
one of 180 single-file write/attr watches; each LOW, 900 B/event. Collectively minor vs execve.

suspected_trap: Two traps. (1) Parse performance / output flood: a naive impl dumps all 212 rules in a flat unranked list, burying the one rule (-S execve) that actually drives ~$9/mo of the ~$20.06/mo total. The correct output RANKS by volume and surfaces the dominant execve rule; the 200 single-file watches are collectively minor (~$5.47/mo). (2) Equal-weighting: a model that tiers all 200 watches the same as the execve rule over-states their collective importance. The ranking logic (f3 section 5.1, 'reliably RANK rules by expected volume') is the load-bearing behavior.
